name: Troglodyte

traits.name: Chameleon Skin
traits.desc: The troglodyte has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide.

traits.name: Stench
traits.desc: Any creature that starts its turn within 5 ft. of the troglodyte must succeed on a DC 12 Con save or be poisoned until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature is immune to the troglodyte's Stench for 24 hours.

traits.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
traits.desc: While in sunlight, the troglodyte has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Perception checks that rely on sight.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The troglodyte makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4+2) piercing damage.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4+2) slashing damage.

tactics: Three attacks per turn. Stench poisons adjacent creatures passively. Sunlight weakness means dungeons and caves are their natural ground.
actionEconomy: Three attacks (bite + 2 claws) per action. Stench is passive.
encounterNotes: Underground caves and ruins. Tribal groups of 10–20. Often keep pets like giant lizards.

name: Nothic

traits.name: Keen Sight
traits.desc: The nothic has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The nothic makes two claw attacks.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

actions.name: Rotting Gaze
actions.desc: The nothic targets one creature it can see within 30 ft. The target must succeed on a DC 12 Con save against this magic or take 10 (3d6) necrotic damage.

actions.name: Weird Insight
actions.desc: The nothic targets one creature it can see within 30 ft. The target must contest its Charisma (Deception) check against the nothic's Wisdom (Insight) check. If the nothic wins, it magically learns one fact or secret about the target. The target automatically wins if it is immune to being charmed.

tactics: Rotting Gaze for ranged necrotic damage, two claw attacks in melee. Weird Insight reveals secrets — use for roleplay leverage.
actionEconomy: Two claw attacks plus Rotting Gaze. Weird Insight for information.
encounterNotes: Dungeons, especially wizard lairs. Once-wizards driven mad by forbidden knowledge. Great roleplay potential.

name: Piercer

traits.name: False Appearance
traits.desc: While the piercer remains motionless on the ceiling, it is indistinguishable from a normal stalactite.

traits.name: Spider Climb
traits.desc: The piercer can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

actions.name: Drop
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, one creature directly below the piercer. Hit: 3 (1d6) piercing damage per 10 feet fallen, to a maximum of 21 (6d6). Miss: The piercer takes half the damage it would have dealt.

tactics: Motionless on ceiling until prey walks beneath, then drops. On a miss it takes damage itself — the main threat is multiple piercers in a cluster.
actionEconomy: Single drop attack. Climbs back up to reset, taking several rounds.
encounterNotes: Caves and dungeons. Cluster of 3–8 piercers on ceiling. Often overlooked until the first one drops.

name: Peryton

traits.name: Dive Attack
traits.desc: If the peryton is flying and dives at least 30 ft. straight toward a target and then hits it with a gore attack, the attack deals an extra 9 (2d8) piercing damage to the target.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The peryton makes one gore attack and one talon attack.

actions.name: Gore
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8+3) piercing damage.

actions.name: Talons
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4+3) piercing damage.

tactics: Dive for extra gore damage, then two attacks. Prefers the hearts of humanoids as trophies.
actionEconomy: Two attacks (gore + talons) plus dive bonus on opening strike.
encounterNotes: Mountains and hills. Hunts in pairs. Has a grotesque hunger for humanoid hearts.

name: Quaggoth

traits.name: Wounded Fury
traits.desc: While it has 10 hit points or fewer, the quaggoth has advantage on attack rolls. In addition, it deals an extra 7 (2d6) damage to any target it hits with a melee attack.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The quaggoth makes two claw attacks.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

tactics: Two claw attacks normally. Wounded Fury below 10 HP makes it MORE dangerous when nearly dead — a desperate, terrifying burst.
actionEconomy: Two claw attacks per action. Wounded Fury passive below 10 HP.
encounterNotes: Underdark. Drow slaves and shock troops. A nearly-dead quaggoth is the real threat.

name: Hook Horror

traits.name: Echolocation
traits.desc: The hook horror can't use its blindsight while deafened.

traits.name: Keen Hearing
traits.desc: The hook horror has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The hook horror makes two hook attacks.

actions.name: Hook
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

tactics: Two 10 ft. reach hook attacks. Climbs walls and ceilings. Communicates via clicking sounds — other hook horrors in earshot will come.
actionEconomy: Two hook attacks per action.
encounterNotes: Underdark caverns. Hunts in packs of 3–6. Clicking communication means the fight can escalate rapidly.

name: Jackalwere
damageResistances: bludgeoning/piercing/slashing from nonmagical attacks not made with silvered weapons

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: The jackalwere can use its action to polymorph into a jackal-humanoid hybrid or into a jackal, or back into its true form. Its statistics are the same in each form.

traits.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
traits.desc: The jackalwere has advantage on Perception checks relying on hearing or smell.

traits.name: Pack Tactics
traits.desc: The jackalwere has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one ally is within 5 ft.

actions.name: Multiattack (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only)
actions.desc: The jackalwere makes two attacks: two Scimitar or one Bite and one Scimitar.

actions.name: Bite (Jackal or Hybrid Form Only)
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4+3) piercing damage. If the target is a humanoid, it must succeed on a DC 10 Con save or be cursed with jackalwere lycanthropy.

actions.name: Scimitar (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only)
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

actions.name: Sleep Gaze
actions.desc: The jackalwere gazes at one creature it can see within 30 ft. The target must make a DC 10 Wis save. On a failure, the target falls unconscious for 10 minutes or until shaken awake or damaged. Only affects humanoids. Recharges after a long rest.

tactics: Uses Sleep Gaze first to drop a party member, then attacks with pack tactics. Disguises as human travelers to ambush caravans.
actionEconomy: Sleep Gaze, then two attacks with pack tactics advantage.
encounterNotes: Desert trade routes. Disguised as merchants or travelers. Gnoll allies sometimes nearby.

name: Gnoll Pack Lord

traits.name: Rampage
traits.desc: When the gnoll reduces a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack on its turn, the gnoll can take a bonus action to move up to half its speed and make a bite attack.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The gnoll pack lord makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its flail or two with its longbow.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) piercing damage.

actions.name: Flail
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8+3) bludgeoning damage.

actions.name: Longbow
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8+2) piercing damage.

bonusActions.name: Incite Rampage
bonusActions.desc: One gnoll the pack lord can see within 30 ft. can use its reaction to make one melee attack.

tactics: Three attacks plus Rampage after kills. Bonus action triggers a nearby gnoll's reaction attack. Commands the warband from mid-range.
actionEconomy: Three attacks per action, Rampage bonus, Incite Rampage bonus action.
encounterNotes: Gnoll warbands. Leads 6–12 gnolls. Most dangerous gnoll non-boss.

name: Gnoll Fang of Yeenoghu

traits.name: Rampage
traits.desc: When the gnoll reduces a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack on its turn, the gnoll can take a bonus action to move up to half its speed and make a bite attack.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The gnoll makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its flail.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 12 Con save or take 7 (2d6) poison damage.

actions.name: Flail
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8+3) bludgeoning damage.

tactics: Three attacks per turn plus Rampage. Demonic gnoll blessed by Yeenoghu — born when a hyena eats a corpse killed by a gnoll. Elite shock troop.
actionEconomy: Three attacks per action plus Rampage bonus.
encounterNotes: Elite gnoll warbands. The most feared gnoll. Serves as champion of Yeenoghu's will.

name: Hobgoblin Warlord

traits.name: Martial Advantage
traits.desc: Once per turn, the hobgoblin can deal an extra 14 (4d6) damage to a creature it hits with a weapon attack if that creature is within 5 ft. of an ally of the hobgoblin that isn't incapacitated.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The hobgoblin makes three melee attacks. Alternatively, it can make two ranged attacks with its javelins.

actions.name: Longsword
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8+3) slashing damage, or 8 (1d10+3) if two-handed.

actions.name: Shield Bash
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 5 (1d4+3) bludgeoning damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a DC 14 Str save or be knocked prone.

actions.name: Javelin
actions.desc: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) piercing damage.

bonusActions.name: Leadership (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
bonusActions.desc: For 1 minute, the hobgoblin can utter a special command or warning whenever a nonhostile creature that it can see within 30 ft. of it makes an attack roll or a saving throw. That creature can add a d4 to its roll.

reactions.name: Parry
reactions.desc: The hobgoblin adds 3 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the hobgoblin must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

tactics: Three attacks per turn. Martial Advantage deals +4d6 when flanking. Shield Bash knocks prone. Parry reaction. Leadership buffs all allies. AC 20 is very high.
actionEconomy: Three attacks per action, Parry reaction, Leadership bonus action.
encounterNotes: Hobgoblin army commander. Leads a military force. The pinnacle of hobgoblin discipline.

name: Cambion

traits.name: Fiendish Blessing
traits.desc: The AC of the cambion includes its Charisma bonus.

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting
traits.desc: Cha-based (spell save DC 14). 3/day each: alter self, command, detect magic. 1/day each: plane shift (self only), cure wounds.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The cambion makes two melee attacks or uses its Fire Ray twice.

actions.name: Spear
actions.desc: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6+4) piercing damage, or 8 (1d8+4) if used with two hands, plus 3 (1d6) fire damage.

actions.name: Fire Ray
actions.desc: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (3d6) fire damage.

actions.name: Etherealness
actions.desc: The cambion magically enters the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa.

tactics: Two spear attacks with fire damage, or two fire rays at range. Fiendish Blessing = AC 19. Plane Shift to escape. Command spell to turn a party member.
actionEconomy: Two attacks per action — melee or ranged fire rays.
encounterNotes: Offspring of fiends and mortals. Scheming masterminds. Often found in cults or devil's courts.

name: Sahuagin Baron

traits.name: Blood Frenzy
traits.desc: The sahuagin has advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its hit points.

traits.name: Limited Amphibiousness
traits.desc: Can breathe air and water but must be submerged at least once every 4 hours or begin to suffocate.

traits.name: Shark Telepathy
traits.desc: Can magically command any shark within 120 ft. using limited telepathy.

traits.name: Four Arms
traits.desc: The sahuagin can make four attacks: two with its claws and two with its bite or trident.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The sahuagin makes four attacks: two with its claws and two with its tridents, or two with its claws and two with its bite.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4+4) piercing damage.

actions.name: Claws
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d4+4) slashing damage.

actions.name: Trident
actions.desc: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

tactics: Four attacks per turn is exceptional action economy. Blood frenzy advantage on any injured target. Commands sharks. Terrifying in water.
actionEconomy: Four attacks per action. Blood frenzy activates early.
encounterNotes: Sahuagin kingdoms underwater. The baron rules a sea-cave or ship-graveyard domain.

name: Sahuagin Priestess

traits.name: Blood Frenzy
traits.desc: Advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its hit points.

traits.name: Limited Amphibiousness
traits.desc: Can breathe air and water; must submerge once every 4 hours.

traits.name: Shark Telepathy
traits.desc: Can magically command any shark within 120 ft.

traits.name: Spellcasting
traits.desc: Wis-based (spell save DC 11). At will: thaumaturgy. 2/day each: bless, detect magic, guiding bolt. 1/day each: hold person, spiritual weapon.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes two attacks with her claws or with her death prayer.

actions.name: Claws
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d4+1) slashing damage.

actions.name: Death Prayer
actions.desc: Ranged Spell Attack: +4 to hit, range 30 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d6) necrotic damage.

tactics: Support caster. Spiritual weapon for ongoing damage, bless to buff allies, hold person to lock down a PC. Blood frenzy for desperate melee.
actionEconomy: Spellcasting priority. Spiritual weapon each turn, guiding bolt on key targets.
encounterNotes: Sahuagin raiding parties. Healer and religious leader. Always found with sahuagin warriors.

name: Lizardfolk Shaman

traits.name: Hold Breath
traits.desc: Can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

traits.name: Spellcasting
traits.desc: Wis-based (spell save DC 12). At will: druidcraft, mending, poison spray. 2/day each: animal friendship, thunderwave. 1/day each: conjure animals, meld into stone, sleet storm.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes two attacks with its claws or uses Conjure Animals.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4+2) slashing damage.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6+2) piercing damage.

tactics: Sleet storm for massive area control. Conjure Animals brings giant lizards or crocodiles. Meld into stone for escape. Poison spray at range.
actionEconomy: Spellcasting priority. Sleet storm changes the whole battle.
encounterNotes: Lizardfolk tribes. Spiritual leader who talks to the spirits of the swamp. Powerful controller.

name: Lizardfolk King/Queen

traits.name: Hold Breath
traits.desc: Can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

traits.name: Spellcasting
traits.desc: Wis-based (spell save DC 12). 2/day each: animal friendship, suggestion. 1/day each: dominate beast, hypnotic pattern.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes three attacks: one with its bite, one with its claws, and one with its tail.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) piercing damage.

actions.name: Claws
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4+3) slashing damage.

actions.name: Tail
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) bludgeoning damage.

tactics: Three attacks per turn plus hypnotic pattern or dominate beast. Commands the whole tribe. Suggestion to manipulate diplomatically.
actionEconomy: Three attacks per action plus spellcasting.
encounterNotes: Lizardfolk settlements. Chief and living god of the tribe.

name: Beholder Zombie
languages: Understands Deep Speech and Undercommon but can't speak

traits.name: Undead Fortitude
traits.desc: If damage reduces the zombie to 0 hit points, it must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 5+the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, the zombie drops to 1 hit point instead.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (4d6) piercing damage.

actions.name: Eye Ray
actions.desc: The zombie shoots one of the following magical eye rays at random (roll a d10), choosing one target it can see within 60 ft. of it. 1–Paralyzing Ray: DC 14 Con save or paralyzed for 1 minute. 2–Fear Ray: DC 14 Wis save or frightened for 1 minute. 3–Enervation Ray: DC 14 Con save, take 36 (8d8) necrotic damage on fail or half on success. 4–Disintegration Ray: DC 14 Dex save or take 45 (10d8) force damage. If this reduces the target to 0 HP, it is disintegrated. 5–10–Death Ray: DC 14 Dex save or take 55 (10d10) necrotic damage.

tactics: Random eye ray is unpredictable and potentially devastating. Death Ray can outright kill. Undead Fortitude makes it hard to drop. Hovers slowly.
actionEconomy: Bite plus one eye ray per action. Random ray keeps players guessing.
encounterNotes: Beholder lairs after the beholder is defeated. Other undead encounters. The eye rays alone make it terrifying.

name: Revenant
languages: The languages it knew in life

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: The revenant regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn. If it takes fire or radiant damage, this trait doesn't function until the start of the revenant's next turn. The revenant's body is destroyed only if it starts its turn with 0 hit points and doesn't regenerate.

traits.name: Rejuvenation
traits.desc: When the revenant's body is destroyed, its soul lingers. After 24 hours, the revenant returns to life in its corpse (or a humanoid corpse within 1 mile if the original is unavailable). This continues until the revenant's vengeance is fulfilled, or a priest makes a DC 15 Wis check to release the soul.

traits.name: Turn Immunity
traits.desc: The revenant is immune to effects that turn undead.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The revenant makes two fist attacks.

actions.name: Fist
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6+4) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature against whom the revenant has a vengeance vow, the target must succeed on a DC 14 Con save or be paralyzed until the end of the revenant's next turn.

actions.name: Horrifying Visage
actions.desc: Each non-undead creature within 60 ft. that can see the revenant must succeed on a DC 15 Wis save or be frightened for 1 minute. Immunity lasts 24 hours on a success.

actions.name: Vengeful Glare
actions.desc: The revenant targets one creature it can see within 30 ft. and against whom it has a vengeance vow. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Wis save or be paralyzed until the revenant deals damage to it or until the end of the revenant's next turn. On a successful save, the target is immune to Vengeful Glare for 24 hours.

tactics: Regenerates 10 HP per turn. Vengeful Glare paralyzes its target of vengeance. Horrifying Visage frightens all around it. Nearly impossible to permanently kill without fulfilling its vow.
actionEconomy: Two fist attacks. Vengeful Glare and Horrifying Visage are the real weapons.
encounterNotes: Adventure hook machine. Always pursuing a specific person. Party may be caught in between, or hired to protect the target.

name: Grick Alpha

traits.name: Stone Camouflage
traits.desc: The grick has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in rocky terrain.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The grick alpha makes two attacks: one with its tentacles and one with its bite.

actions.name: Tentacles
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d8+4) slashing damage.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8+4) piercing damage.

tactics: Two attacks with brutal tentacle damage. Resistance to nonmagical weapons. Stone camouflage for devastating ambushes. Much larger and more dangerous than the standard grick.
actionEconomy: Two attacks per action (tentacles + bite).
encounterNotes: Caves and dungeons. Commands a pack of regular gricks. The alpha hangs back until its minions are in danger.

name: Galeb Duhr

traits.name: False Appearance
traits.desc: While the galeb duhr remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal boulder.

traits.name: Rolling Charge
traits.desc: If the galeb duhr rolls at least 20 ft. straight toward a target and hits it with a slam attack, the target takes an extra 9 (2d8) bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a DC 16 Str save or be knocked prone.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The galeb duhr makes two slam attacks.

actions.name: Slam
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6+5) bludgeoning damage.

actions.name: Animate Boulders
actions.desc: The galeb duhr magically animates up to two boulders it can see within 60 ft. of it. A boulder has the statistics of a galeb duhr, except it has Intelligence 1, Wisdom 1, Charisma 1, and it lacks this action and the Rolling Charge trait. The boulders obey the galeb duhr's commands. The boulders remain animated for 1 minute, until the galeb duhr dies, or until the galeb duhr dismisses one or both of them as a bonus action.

tactics: False appearance for ambush. Rolling charge for knockdown. Animate Boulders effectively triples its threat. Two slam attacks.
actionEconomy: Two slam attacks plus Animate Boulders for reinforcements.
encounterNotes: Mountain passes and boulder fields. Earth elemental guardians. May negotiate if treated with respect.

name: Cyclops

traits.name: Poor Depth Perception
traits.desc: The cyclops has disadvantage on any attack roll against a target more than 30 ft. away.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The cyclops makes two greatclub attacks.

actions.name: Greatclub
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (3d8+6) bludgeoning damage.

actions.name: Rock
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (4d10+6) bludgeoning damage. On a hit, the target must succeed on a DC 17 Str save or be knocked prone.

tactics: Two greatclub attacks with 10 ft. reach. Rock throws knock prone. Poor Depth Perception disadvantage beyond 30 ft. — keep close or use spells at range.
actionEconomy: Two greatclub attacks per action. Rock for ranged knockdown.
encounterNotes: Island shores and mountain valleys. Reclusive but territorial. Commands sheep, giant goats, or worse.

name: Blue Slaad

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: The slaad regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: The slaad has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The slaad makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6+5) piercing damage.

actions.name: Claws
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6+5) slashing damage. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Con save. On a failure, the target is infected with a disease — a slaad egg. A humanoid host can carry only one slaad egg at a time. Over 3 months, the egg moves to the chest cavity, and the host has disadvantage on attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks. At the end of the 3 months, the egg hatches, and a red slaad bursts from the host's chest, killing it.

tactics: Three attacks plus disease implantation. Regeneration sustains it. Magic resistance frustrates casters. The real horror is the long-term disease.
actionEconomy: Three attacks (bite + 2 claws) per action. Claws implant slaad eggs.
encounterNotes: Limbo and planar chaos. The claw disease is a campaign-level threat — find a cleric fast.

name: Yuan-ti Abomination

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting
traits.desc: Cha-based (spell save DC 15). At will: animal friendship (snakes only), message, minor illusion, prestidigitation. 3/day: fear, suggestion. 1/day each: polymorph.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: Can use action to polymorph into a Large snake, or back into its true form. Statistics unchanged.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes two attacks: one with its constrict and one with its scimitar or longbow.

actions.name: Constrict
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6+4) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 14). Until this grapple ends, the creature is restrained, and the abomination can't constrict another target.

actions.name: Scimitar
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d4+6) slashing damage.

actions.name: Longbow
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8+3) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) poison damage.

tactics: Constrict to restrain, scimitar to hit. Longbow with massive poison damage at range. Fear and suggestion for control. Magic resistance. The most powerful yuan-ti.
actionEconomy: Two attacks per action (constrict + weapon). Spellcasting for control.
encounterNotes: Yuan-ti temple hearts. The rulers of yuan-ti society. They answer only to their dark god.

name: Fomorian

traits.name: Curse of the Evil Eye
traits.desc: As a bonus action, the fomorian attempts to curse one creature it can see within 60 ft. The target must succeed on a DC 14 Cha save or be cursed until it finishes a long rest. Until the curse ends, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks, and it can't regain hit points. The fomorian can have only one creature cursed in this way at a time.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The fomorian makes two greatclub attacks.

actions.name: Greatclub
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (3d8+6) bludgeoning damage.

actions.name: Corrupting Gaze
actions.desc: The fomorian targets one creature it can see within 30 ft. The target must succeed on a DC 14 Con save. On a failed save, the target is magically transformed into a random creature — roll on the Polymorph table. The transformation lasts for 1 hour or until the target drops to 0 hit points or dies.

tactics: Two huge greatclub attacks. Evil Eye curse as a bonus action every round. Corrupting Gaze can transform a PC into a random creature. Deep intelligence despite appearance.
actionEconomy: Two attacks per action plus Evil Eye bonus action every turn.
encounterNotes: Underdark. Twisted fey giants cursed by the Seelie Court. Brutal and cunning despite looking bestial.

name: Nycaloth
damageResistances: bludgeoning/piercing/slashing from nonmagical attacks not made with cold iron

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Magic Weapons
traits.desc: The nycaloth's weapon attacks are magical.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The nycaloth makes three attacks: one with its claw and two with its greataxe, or it makes one claw attack and uses Teleport.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8+5) slashing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 16 Con save or be lacerated. A lacerated creature takes 9 (1d8+5) slashing damage at the start of each of its turns. Any creature can take an action to stanch the wound with a successful DC 13 Wis (Medicine) check. The wound also closes if the target receives any magical healing.

actions.name: Greataxe
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d12+5) slashing damage.

actions.name: Teleport
actions.desc: The nycaloth magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 60 ft. to an unoccupied space it can see.

tactics: Three attacks including a claw that causes a bleeding wound. Teleport for positioning. Magic resistance. Summoned by other yugoloths.
actionEconomy: Three attacks per action. Teleport for tactical repositioning.
encounterNotes: Hired mercenary fiends. Often summoned by warlocks or found in yugoloth mercenary companies.

name: Death Knight

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Marshal Undead
traits.desc: Unless the death knight is incapacitated, it and all undead creatures within 60 ft. that can hear it have advantage on saving throws against features that turn undead.

traits.name: Spellcasting
traits.desc: Cha-based (spell save DC 18). At will: detect magic, fireball. 1/day each: circle of death, destructive wave (necrotic), dispel magic, hold monster, storm of vengeance, wall of ice.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The death knight makes three longsword attacks.

actions.name: Longsword
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8+5) slashing damage, or 10 (1d10+5) slashing damage if used with two hands, plus 18 (4d8) necrotic damage.

actions.name: Hellfire Orb (1/Day)
actions.desc: The death knight hurls a magical ball of fire that explodes at a point it can see within 180 ft. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a DC 18 Dex save. The sphere spreads around corners. A creature takes 35 (10d6) fire damage and 35 (10d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half on a successful one.

reactions.name: Parry
reactions.desc: The death knight adds 6 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it.

tactics: Three longsword attacks with +18 necrotic each. Hellfire Orb once per day for massive AoE. Storm of vengeance as ultimate nuclear option. Buffs nearby undead. Parry reaction keeps it alive.
actionEconomy: Three attacks per action, Parry reaction, spellcasting for burst damage.
encounterNotes: The fallen paladin. Epic encounter. Has a tragic backstory. Sworn to an evil deity.

name: Mind Flayer

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

actions.name: Tentacles
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 15 (2d10+4) psychic damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it is grappled (escape DC 15) and must succeed on a DC 15 Int save or be stunned until this grapple ends.

actions.name: Extract Brain
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one incapacitated humanoid grappled by the mind flayer. Hit: 55 (10d10) piercing damage. If this damage reduces the target to 0 hit points, the mind flayer kills the target by extracting and devouring its brain.

actions.name: Mind Blast (Recharge 5–6)
actions.desc: The mind flayer magically emits psychic energy in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 15 Int save or take 22 (4d8+4) psychic damage and be stunned for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

tactics: Tentacles to grapple and stun, then Extract Brain for instant death. Mind Blast stuns the whole group. Magic resistance. Psionic overlord.
actionEconomy: Tentacle attack to grapple+stun, Mind Blast on recharge for AoE stun.
encounterNotes: Underdark. Elder brain controls them telepathically. Mind flayer colonies are civilization-ending threats.

name: Mind Flayer Arcanist

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Spellcasting
traits.desc: Int-based (spell save DC 15). At will: detect magic, levitate, mage hand, prestidigitation, detect thoughts. 2/day each: dominate monster, plane shift. 1/day each: feeblemind, imprisonment, power word stun.

actions.name: Tentacles
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 15 (2d10+4) psychic damage. If Medium or smaller, grappled (DC 15) and stunned on failed DC 15 Int save until grapple ends.

actions.name: Extract Brain
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one incapacitated humanoid grappled. Hit: 55 (10d10) piercing damage. Kills target by brain extraction on 0 HP.

actions.name: Mind Blast (Recharge 5–6)
actions.desc: 60-foot cone. DC 15 Int save or take 22 (4d8+4) psychic damage and be stunned for 1 minute.

tactics: Same as mind flayer plus devastating wizard spells. Dominate Monster, Feeblemind, Power Word Stun. The most dangerous Underdark spellcaster.
actionEconomy: Tentacle/Extract Brain plus full spellcasting list. Mind Blast on recharge.
encounterNotes: Mind flayer colonies. The arcanist advises the elder brain. A solo encounter for a well-prepared high-level party.

name: Flumph
languages: Understands Undercommon but communicates via telepathy (60 ft.)

traits.name: Advanced Telepathy
traits.desc: The flumph can perceive the content of any telepathic communication used within 60 ft. of it, and it can't be surprised by creatures with telepathic abilities.

traits.name: Prone Deficiency
traits.desc: If the flumph is knocked prone, roll a d20. On an 11 or higher, the flumph is upright at the end of its turn. Otherwise, it remains prone.

traits.name: Telepathic Shroud
traits.desc: The flumph is immune to any effect that would sense its emotions or read its thoughts, as well as to all divination spells.

actions.name: Tendrils
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 4 (1d4+2) piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) acid damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 10 Con save or reek of musk. While reeking, the target has disadvantage on Charisma checks. The musk dissipates after the target takes a bath.

actions.name: Stench Spray (1/Day)
actions.desc: Each creature in a 15-foot line must succeed on a DC 12 Str save or be coated in smelly liquid. A coated creature reeks of the liquid for 8 hours. The coated creature has disadvantage on Charisma checks. Creatures that can smell detect the coated creature at up to triple the normal distance.

tactics: Rarely fights. Telepathically shares information. Stench Spray as a last resort. Best used as an unexpected ally offering Underdark intelligence.
actionEconomy: Tendrils or Stench Spray. Mostly a social/information creature.
encounterNotes: Underdark. Spies on mind flayer colonies and shares what they learn. Potentially the most valuable ally in the Underdark.

name: Beholder

traits.name: Antimagic Cone
traits.desc: The beholder's central eye creates an area of antimagic, as in the antimagic field spell, in a 150-foot cone. At the start of each of its turns, the beholder decides which way the cone faces and whether the cone is active. The area works against the beholder's own eye rays.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (4d6) piercing damage.

actions.name: Eye Rays
actions.desc: The beholder shoots three of the following magical eye rays at random (reroll duplicates), choosing one to three targets it can see within 120 ft. 1-Charm Ray: DC 16 Wis save or charmed for 1 hour. 2-Paralyzing Ray: DC 16 Con save or paralyzed for 1 minute. 3-Fear Ray: DC 16 Wis save or frightened for 1 minute. 4-Slowing Ray: DC 16 Dex save or speed halved, -2 AC and Dex saves, no reactions, only one action or bonus per turn, for 1 minute. 5-Enervation Ray: DC 16 Con save, 36 (8d8) necrotic on fail or half on success. 6-Telekinetic Ray: DC 16 Str save or moved 30 ft. in any direction and restrained. 7-Sleep Ray: DC 16 Wis save or unconscious for 1 minute. 8-Petrification Ray: DC 16 Dex save, restrained on fail; petrified on second fail. 9-Disintegration Ray: DC 16 Dex save or 45 (10d8) force damage; disintegrated if reduced to 0 HP. 10-Death Ray: DC 16 Dex save or 55 (10d10) necrotic; instant death if reduced to 0 HP.

legendaryActions.name: Eye Ray
legendaryActions.desc: The beholder uses one random eye ray.

legendaryActionsNote: The beholder can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn.
tactics: Antimagic Cone nullifies all magic in the area (aim at spellcasters). Shoots 3 random eye rays per action plus 1 per legendary. Death Ray or Disintegration Ray can end a PC. Paranoid and solitary — the lair is a death trap.
actionEconomy: Three eye rays per action plus legendary eye rays. Antimagic Cone is the defining tactical tool.
encounterNotes: Beholder lairs in underground caverns. The most iconic D&D monster. Its lair is customized to its paranoid mind.

name: Vampire
languages: The languages it knew in life

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: The vampire can use its action to polymorph into a Tiny bat or a Medium cloud of mist, or back into its true form. Statistics unchanged except as noted.

traits.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
traits.desc: If the vampire fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

traits.name: Misty Escape
traits.desc: When it drops to 0 HP outside its resting place, the vampire transforms into a cloud of mist (if it isn't in running water or sunlight) instead of falling unconscious. If it can't transform, it is destroyed. While it has 0 HP in mist form, it can't revert to its vampire form, and it must reach its resting place within 2 hours or be destroyed. While in mist form, it can't take any actions, speak, or manipulate objects. It is weightless, has a flying speed of 20 ft., can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there, and can pass through any opening that air can pass through. It is immune to all damage except the damage it takes from sunlight.

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: The vampire regains 20 hit points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point and isn't in sunlight or running water. If the vampire takes radiant damage or damage from holy water, this trait doesn't function at the start of the vampire's next turn.

traits.name: Spider Climb
traits.desc: The vampire can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

traits.name: Vampire Weaknesses
traits.desc: Forbiddance — can't enter a residence without invitation. Harmed by Running Water — takes 20 acid damage per turn in running water. Stake to the Heart — paralyzed with stake through heart while it remains. Sunlight Hypersensitivity — 20 radiant damage per turn in sunlight, disadvantage on attacks and checks.

actions.name: Multiattack (Vampire Form Only)
actions.desc: The vampire makes two attacks, only one of which can be a bite attack.

actions.name: Unarmed Strike
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 8 (1d8+4) bludgeoning damage. Instead of dealing damage, the vampire can grapple the target (escape DC 18).

actions.name: Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only)
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one willing creature, or a creature that is grappled by the vampire, incapacitated, or restrained. Hit: 7 (1d6+4) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage. The target's hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains hit points equal to that amount. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hit point maximum to 0. A humanoid slain in this way and then buried in the ground rises the following night as a vampire spawn under the vampire's control.

actions.name: Charm
actions.desc: The vampire targets one humanoid it can see within 30 ft. If the target can see the vampire, the target must succeed on a DC 17 Wis save against this magic or be charmed by the vampire. The charmed target regards the vampire as a trusted friend to be heeded and protected. Each time the vampire or the vampire's companions do anything harmful to the target, it can repeat the saving throw. Otherwise, the charm lasts 24 hours or until the vampire is destroyed, is on a different plane of existence from the target, or takes a bonus action to end the effect.

actions.name: Children of the Night (1/Day)
actions.desc: The vampire magically calls 2d4 swarms of bats or rats, provided the sun isn't up. While outdoors, the vampire can call 3d6 wolves instead. The creatures arrive in 1d4 rounds, acting as allies of the vampire and obeying its spoken commands. The beasts remain for 1 hour, until the vampire dies, or until the vampire dismisses them as a bonus action.

legendaryActions.name: Move
legendaryActions.desc: The vampire moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendaryActions.name: Unarmed Strike
legendaryActions.desc: The vampire makes one unarmed strike.

legendaryActions.name: Bite (Costs 2 Actions)
legendaryActions.desc: The vampire makes one bite attack.

legendaryActionsNote: The vampire can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn.
tactics: Charm to control a party member. Grapple then bite for HP max reduction. Misty Escape makes it nearly impossible to kill outside its lair. Legendary resistance eats saving throws.
actionEconomy: Two attacks per action, Charm as a devastating control tool, legendary actions for extra attacks.
encounterNotes: Gothic horror campaigns. The vampire lord in its castle. Requires careful planning to defeat permanently.

name: Yuan-ti Malison (Type 1)

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: Can polymorph into a Medium snake or back. Statistics unchanged.

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting
traits.desc: Cha-based (spell save DC 13). At will: animal friendship (snakes only), message, minor illusion, prestidigitation. 3/day: suggestion.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

actions.name: Multiattack (Humanoid or Half-Serpent Form)
actions.desc: Makes two ranged attacks or two melee attacks.

actions.name: Scimitar (Humanoid or Half-Serpent Form Only)
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

actions.name: Longbow (Humanoid Form Only)
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8+1) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

actions.name: Bite (Snake Form Only)
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4+3) piercing damage. If the target is a humanoid, it must succeed on a DC 11 Con save or become poisoned for 1 hour. If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the target is also unconscious while poisoned. The target wakes up if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.

tactics: Two attacks with suggestion for mind control. Human upper body / snake lower body. Infiltrators and cultists.
actionEconomy: Two attacks per action. Suggestion 3/day is the key tool.
encounterNotes: Yuan-ti temples. Guards and cultists of Merrshaulk. Type 1 has human head and lower snake body.

name: Yuan-ti Malison (Type 2)

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: Can polymorph into a Medium snake or back. Statistics unchanged.

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting
traits.desc: Cha-based (spell save DC 13). At will: animal friendship (snakes only), message, minor illusion, prestidigitation. 3/day: suggestion.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes two ranged attacks or two melee attacks.

actions.name: Scimitar
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

actions.name: Longbow
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8+1) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

actions.name: Constrict
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6+3) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 13). Until this grapple ends, the creature is restrained, and the malison can't constrict another target.

tactics: Same as Type 1 but with a constrict attack. Has a human head with a snake body replacing legs — Type 2.
actionEconomy: Two attacks per action. Constrict to restrain. Suggestion for control.
encounterNotes: Yuan-ti temples. Type 2 has human head and snake body below the waist — longer reach with constrict.

name: Yuan-ti Malison (Type 3)

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: Can polymorph into a Medium snake or back. Statistics unchanged.

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting
traits.desc: Cha-based (spell save DC 13). At will: animal friendship (snakes only), message, minor illusion, prestidigitation. 3/day: suggestion.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes two ranged attacks or two melee attacks.

actions.name: Scimitar
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

actions.name: Longbow
actions.desc: Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8+1) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

actions.name: Snake Heads
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4+3) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 11 Con save or take 11 (2d10) poison damage.

tactics: Same as Types 1/2 but with snake heads for poisonous bite. Has human body with snake heads in place of hands — Type 3.
actionEconomy: Two attacks per action. Snake Heads for poison. Suggestion for control.
encounterNotes: Yuan-ti temples. Type 3 has a humanoid body but snake heads replacing its hands.

name: Archmage

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

traits.name: Spellcasting
traits.desc: Int-based (spell save DC 17). Cantrips: fire bolt, light, mage hand, prestidigitation, shocking grasp. 1st (4 slots): detect magic, identify, mage armor, magic missile. 2nd (3 slots): detect thoughts, mirror image, misty step. 3rd (3 slots): counterspell, fly, lightning bolt. 4th (3 slots): banishment, fire shield, stoneskin. 5th (3 slots): cone of cold, scrying, wall of force. 6th (1 slot): globe of invulnerability. 7th (1 slot): teleport. 8th (1 slot): mind blank. 9th (1 slot): time stop.

actions.name: Dagger
actions.desc: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4+2) piercing damage.

tactics: Time Stop then layer defenses and offense. Globe of Invulnerability makes it immune to spells of 5th level or lower. Mind blank pre-cast to be immune to divination. Counter everything with Counterspell.
actionEconomy: Full 9th-level spellcaster. Dagger is a last resort.
encounterNotes: The most powerful wizard NPC. Ally, patron, or final boss of a magic-heavy campaign.

name: Glabrezu

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting
traits.desc: Cha-based (spell save DC 15). At will: darkness, detect magic, dispel magic. 1/day each: confusion, fly, power word stun.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Magic Weapons
traits.desc: The glabrezu's weapon attacks are magical.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The glabrezu makes four attacks: two with its pincers and two with its fists. Alternatively, it makes two attacks with its pincers and casts one spell.

actions.name: Pincer
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d10+5) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 15). The glabrezu has two pincers, each of which can grapple only one target.

actions.name: Fist
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d4+2) bludgeoning damage.

tactics: Four attacks per turn or two attacks plus a spell. Grabs with pincers and punches the grappled target. Power word stun or confusion to control. Magic resistance.
actionEconomy: Four attacks per action OR two pincers plus one spell. Extremely high action economy.
encounterNotes: Demon lord servants. Often found fulfilling contracts with mortals. Deceivers who twist wishes.

name: Green Slaad

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: Regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: Can use action to polymorph into a Small or Medium humanoid, or back. Statistics unchanged except size.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws or uses Hurl Flame twice.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6+4) piercing damage.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6+4) slashing damage.

actions.name: Hurl Flame
actions.desc: Ranged Spell Attack: +3 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (3d6) fire damage. The fire ignites flammable objects in the target's space that aren't being worn or carried.

tactics: Three attacks or two fire blasts. Shapechanger for infiltration. Regeneration. Magic resistance. Transforms from tadpole implant of red/blue slaad.
actionEconomy: Three attacks per action or two Hurl Flame attacks.
encounterNotes: Evolved form of red or blue slaad. Eventually transforms again into gray slaad.

name: Red Slaad

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: Regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4+3) piercing damage.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage. If the target is a humanoid, it must succeed on a DC 14 Con save or be infected with a disease — a slaad egg that hatches in 24 hours into a red slaad, bursting from the host's body and killing it. Only one egg at a time.

tactics: Three attacks. Claw implants a rapidly-hatching egg — 24 hours until a new slaad erupts from the victim. Chaos incarnate.
actionEconomy: Three attacks (bite + 2 claws) per action. Claw disease is the main threat.
encounterNotes: Limbo and chaotic planes. The egg incubation is much faster than a blue slaad — days instead of months.

name: Gray Slaad

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: Regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: Can use its action to polymorph into a Small or Medium humanoid, or back.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes three attacks with its claws.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6+3) slashing damage. If the target is a humanoid, it must succeed on a DC 15 Con save or be infected with a disease that transforms it. At the start of each of the target's turns, the target must repeat the saving throw, losing 10 (3d6) hit points and taking 5 (1d10) Con damage on a failure. The target dies if this reduces its Constitution to 0. A humanoid slain by this disease transforms into a gray slaad. A humanoid infected for 1 hour and then cured is immune to this slaad's disease for 24 hours.

tactics: Three claw attacks with rapidly lethal disease. Shapechanger for infiltration. The highest natural slaad form before death slaad. Very difficult to deal with at the table.
actionEconomy: Three claw attacks per action. Disease is a slow death without curing.
encounterNotes: Limbo. Evolved from green slaad. Commands lesser slaadi.

name: Death Slaad

traits.name: Regeneration
traits.desc: Regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Shapechanger
traits.desc: Can use its action to polymorph into a Small or Medium humanoid, or back.

traits.name: Innate Spellcasting
traits.desc: Cha-based (spell save DC 15). At will: detect magic, detect thoughts, fireball. 2/day each: fear, fly, tongues. 1/day each: circle of death, cloudkill, power word blind.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: Makes three attacks with its claws.

actions.name: Claws
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d4+5) slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) psychic damage.

tactics: Three claw attacks with psychic damage plus a full spell list. Circle of Death for massive AoE. Power Word Blind to cripple. Shapechanger for infiltration. The pinnacle of slaad evolution.
actionEconomy: Three attacks per action plus extensive innate spellcasting.
encounterNotes: Limbo and wherever chaos seeks to destroy. The rarest and most powerful slaad. A major encounter.

name: Empyrean

traits.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
traits.desc: If the empyrean fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Magic Weapons
traits.desc: The empyrean's weapon attacks are magical.

actions.name: Maul
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 31 (6d6+10) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 15 Con save or be stunned until the end of the empyrean's next turn.

actions.name: Bolt (Recharge 5–6)
actions.desc: Ranged Spell Attack: +15 to hit, range 600 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (7d6) damage of a type chosen from: acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, radiant, or thunder.

legendaryActions.name: Bolster
legendaryActions.desc: The empyrean bolsters all nonhostile creatures within 120 ft. Until the end of the empyrean's next turn, the targets can add the empyrean's Charisma modifier (+8) to their attack rolls and saving throws.

legendaryActions.name: Trembling Strike (Costs 2 Actions)
legendaryActions.desc: The empyrean strikes the ground, creating a tremor. All other creatures on the ground within 60 ft. must succeed on a DC 25 Str save or be knocked prone and take 14 (4d6) bludgeoning damage.

legendaryActionsNote: The empyrean can take 3 legendary actions.
tactics: Stunning maul attacks. Legendary Bolster gives +8 to all ally rolls. Trembling Strike for massive AoE knockdown. An offspring of gods — not typically a combat encounter but can be.
actionEconomy: Maul attack plus legendary actions. Bolt for ranged burst.
encounterNotes: Celestial planes or when a deity's child descends. Usually a patron or quest-giver, but can be a terrifying foe if evil-aligned.

name: Spectator

traits.name: False Appearance
traits.desc: While the spectator remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary eye-shaped decoration.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 2 (1d6−1) piercing damage.

actions.name: Eye Rays
actions.desc: The spectator shoots two of the following magical eye rays at random (reroll duplicates), choosing one or two targets it can see within 90 ft. 1-Confusion Ray: DC 13 Wis save or affected by confusion until end of next turn. 2-Paralyzing Ray: DC 13 Con save or paralyzed for 1 minute. 3-Fear Ray: DC 13 Wis save or frightened for 1 minute. 4-Wounding Ray: DC 13 Con save, 16 (3d10) necrotic on fail or half on success.

reactions.name: Spell Reflection
reactions.desc: If the spectator makes a successful saving throw against a spell, or a spell attack misses it, the spectator can choose another creature (including the spellcaster) it can see within 30 ft. That creature must succeed on a DC 13 saving throw against the spell or be affected by it as if it were the original target.

tactics: Two random eye rays per action. Spell Reflection bounces spells back at casters. Summoned by beholders to guard treasure. Delusional — may think it's still guarding whatever it was summoned to guard, even if that time has long passed.
actionEconomy: Two eye rays per action. Spell Reflection is a punishing reaction.
encounterNotes: Wizard vaults and beholder lairs. Can be reasoned with if its delusion is respected. A beholder's poor man's guardian.

name: Umber Hulk

traits.name: Confusing Gaze
traits.desc: When a creature starts its turn within 30 ft. of the umber hulk and is able to see the umber hulk's eyes, the umber hulk can magically force it to make a DC 15 Cha save. On a failed save, the creature can't take reactions until the start of its next turn and rolls a d8 to determine what it does during its turn: 1-4, the creature does nothing; 5-6, the creature takes no action or bonus action and uses all its movement to move in a randomly determined direction; 7-8, the creature makes a melee attack against a randomly determined creature within its reach or does nothing if it can't make such an attack.

traits.name: Tunneler
traits.desc: The umber hulk can burrow through solid rock at half its burrow speed and leaves a 5-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The umber hulk makes three attacks: two with its claws and one with its mandibles.

actions.name: Claw
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8+5) slashing damage.

actions.name: Mandibles
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8+5) slashing damage.

tactics: Confusing Gaze requires a save every turn — confused creatures attack randomly or do nothing. Three attacks. Burrows through rock. The Underdark nightmare.
actionEconomy: Three attacks (2 claws + mandibles) per action. Confusing Gaze passive at start of each enemy turn.
encounterNotes: Underdark tunnels. Burrows up under adventurers. Confusing Gaze causes party members to attack each other.

name: Half-Ogre

actions.name: Battleaxe
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8+3) slashing damage, or 14 (2d10+3) slashing damage if used with two hands.

actions.name: Javelin
actions.desc: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6+3) piercing damage.

tactics: Straightforward brute. High damage battleaxe. Used as shock troops alongside orc warbands.
actionEconomy: Single attack per turn.
encounterNotes: Orc warbands. Occasional offspring of orc and ogre. Bridge between humanoid and giant-kin threats.

name: Gauth

traits.name: Death Throes
traits.desc: When the gauth dies, the magical energy within it explodes. Each creature within 10 ft. must succeed on a DC 14 Dex save or take 5 (1d10) force damage plus 5 (1d10) fire damage.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (4d6+2) piercing damage.

actions.name: Eye Rays
actions.desc: The gauth shoots three of the following magical eye rays at random (reroll duplicates), choosing one to three targets it can see within 120 ft. 1-Dazing Ray: DC 13 Con save or incapacitated until end of next turn. 2-Fear Ray: DC 13 Wis save or frightened for 1 minute. 3-Enervation Ray: DC 13 Con save, 22 (5d8) necrotic on fail or half on success. 4-Pushing Ray: DC 13 Str save or pushed 15 ft. away and knocked prone. 5-Stunning Ray: DC 13 Con save or stunned until end of next turn. 6-Weakening Ray: DC 13 Str save, all Str-based rolls at disadvantage for 1 minute on fail.

bonusActions.name: Spell Eating
bonusActions.desc: If the gauth is targeted by a spell, it can eat the spell as a bonus action. The caster must make a DC 14 spellcasting ability check. On a failed check, the gauth eats the spell and regains 5 (1d10) hit points.

tactics: Three eye rays plus Spell Eating to negate spells. Death Throes punishes melee. A lesser beholder-kin that hunts for magical energy to consume.
actionEconomy: Three eye rays per action. Spell Eating as bonus action to counter magic.
encounterNotes: Caves and dungeons. Drawn to magical energy. Eats spells for sustenance.

name: Gazer
languages: Understands Deep Speech but can't speak

traits.name: Mimicry
traits.desc: The gazer can mimic the sounds it has heard, including voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful DC 10 Wis (Insight) check.

traits.name: Aggressive
traits.desc: As a bonus action, the gazer can move up to its speed toward a hostile creature that it can see.

actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1 piercing damage.

actions.name: Eye Rays
actions.desc: The gazer shoots two of the following magical eye rays at random (reroll duplicates), choosing one or two targets it can see within 60 ft. 1-Dazing Ray: DC 11 Wis save or incapacitated until end of next turn. 2-Fear Ray: DC 11 Wis save or frightened until end of next turn. 3-Frost Ray: DC 11 Con save or 10 (3d6) cold damage on fail or half on success. 4-Telekinetic Ray: DC 11 Str save or pushed 10 ft. in any direction on fail.

tactics: Two eye rays per action. Tiny size lets it fit in small spaces. Dazing or Fear ray can shut down a character. Often kept as a familiar by beholders.
actionEconomy: Two eye rays per action. Bite is negligible.
encounterNotes: Beholder lairs. Juvenile beholder-kin. Sometimes kept as familiars by powerful wizards or beholders.

name: Death Tyrant

traits.name: Negative Energy Cone
traits.desc: The death tyrant's central eye emits a 150-foot cone of negative energy. At the start of each of the death tyrant's turns, undead creatures in that area are invigorated: until the start of the death tyrant's next turn, such creatures add 10 (3d6) necrotic damage to their melee weapon attacks. Enemies cannot benefit from healing spells of 6th level or lower while in the cone.

traits.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
traits.desc: If the death tyrant fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

actions.name: Eye Rays
actions.desc: The death tyrant shoots three of the following magical eye rays at random (reroll duplicates), choosing one to three targets it can see within 120 ft. Uses the same rays as the Beholder. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by the death ray rises as a zombie under the death tyrant's control.

legendaryActions.name: Eye Ray
legendaryActions.desc: The death tyrant uses one random eye ray.

legendaryActionsNote: The death tyrant can take 3 legendary actions.
tactics: Negative Energy Cone buffs all nearby undead and prevents enemy healing. Same eye rays as beholder. Creates zombies from kills. The undead evolution of a beholder.
actionEconomy: Three eye rays per action plus legendary eye rays. Negative Energy Cone is passive but game-changing.
encounterNotes: When a beholder refuses to die. Commands an undead army. One of the most dangerous CR 14 creatures in the MM.

name: Elder Brain

traits.name: Creature Sense
traits.desc: The elder brain is aware of creatures within 5 miles of it that have an Intelligence score of 4 or greater. It knows the distance and direction to each creature, as well as each creature's Intelligence score, but can't sense anything else about it. A creature protected by a mind blank spell, a nondetection spell, or similar magic can't be perceived in this manner.

traits.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
traits.desc: If the elder brain fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

traits.name: Magic Resistance
traits.desc: Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.

traits.name: Telepathic Hub
traits.desc: If the elder brain is targeted by a telepathy spell or similar magic, the caster can communicate telepathically with all creatures within 5 miles of the elder brain.

actions.name: Tentacle
actions.desc: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (4d8+2) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 18 Str save or be pulled to within 5 ft. of the elder brain. The target is grappled (escape DC 18) if the elder brain isn't already grappling a creature, and the target is restrained until the grapple ends.

actions.name: Mind Blast (Recharge 5–6)
actions.desc: The elder brain magically emits psychic energy in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 18 Int save or take 32 (5d10+5) psychic damage and be stunned for 1 minute.

actions.name: Psychic Link
actions.desc: The elder brain targets one incapacitated creature it can perceive with its Creature Sense and establishes a psychic link with that creature. Until the psychic link ends, the elder brain can perceive everything the target senses. The target can attempt to break the link by making a DC 18 Cha save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The elder brain can end the link anytime (no action required). The psychic link ends if the target or the elder brain drops to 0 hit points.

legendaryActions.name: Tentacle
legendaryActions.desc: The elder brain makes one tentacle attack.

legendaryActions.name: Break Concentration (Costs 2 Actions)
legendaryActions.desc: The elder brain targets one creature it can perceive with Creature Sense that is concentrating on a spell. The creature must make a DC 18 Con save. On a failed save, the creature's concentration is broken.

legendaryActions.name: Psychic Pulse (Costs 3 Actions)
legendaryActions.desc: Each creature with a psychic link to the elder brain takes 10 (3d6) psychic damage.

legendaryActionsNote: The elder brain can take 3 legendary actions.
tactics: Telepathic awareness of all intelligent creatures within 5 miles. 30-ft. reach tentacles drag victims close. Mind Blast stuns groups. Psychic Link spies through victims. Commands all mind flayers in its colony.
actionEconomy: Tentacle attacks and Mind Blast. Legendary tentacle attacks and Psychic Pulse.
encounterNotes: The heart of a mind flayer colony. Defeating it collapses the colony's hierarchy. An epic campaign climax.
